item.title: Convicted rapist who faked death and fled U.S. has died
item.description: Nicholas Rossi​, 38, was serving at least 10 years in prison in Utah following his convictions in 2025 in two sexual assault cases.

item.title: Former Indiana governor explains how his nonprofit will help workers impacted by AI boom
item.description: RAISE US, a nonprofit group, launched a new fund to help ease the transition for workers impacted by AI. Former Indiana Gov. Eric Holcomb, a co-chair of RAISE US, joins to discuss.

item.title: Billionaire Leon Black subpoenaed to return to House Epstein panel in 3 weeks
item.description: An investor who employed and was close to Jeffrey Epstein appeared before members of Congress Friday investigating the deceased sexual abuser.

item.title: Mangione's attorneys discussed possible plea deal but talks fell apart, sources say
item.description: Mangione​ is facing both state and federal charges for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's murder in December 2024. He has pleaded not guilty in both cases.

item.title: Billionaire Leon Black subpoenaed to return to House Epstein committee, Comer says
item.description: House Oversight Chairman James Comer said he issued two subpoenas to Leon Black after the billionaire refused to answer specific questions during his closed-door testimony before the committee investigating Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: No emergency fund? You may currently own the safety net you need.
item.description: The need for an emergency fund is strong in today's economy. Here's how to access the safety net you may already own.

item.title: Trump accuses Iran of "foolish violation" after ship struck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced a deal Friday between Lebanon and Israel, but Hezbollah was not part of the negotiations.

item.title: Judge declares mistrial in Palisades Fire arson trial after jury deadlocked
item.description: A judge declared a mistrial in the case against a man accused of starting a fire that grew into the deadly 2025 Palisades Fire. The jury was deadlocked during deliberations. CBS News Los Angeles has more.

item.title: Trump accuses Iran of "foolish violation" of ceasefire after drone strike on cargo ship
item.description: President Trump accused Iran of a "foolish violation" of the ceasefire with the U.S. after an Iranian drone struck a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more.

item.title: John Bolton faces prison time after pleading guilty in classified documents case
item.description: Former National Security adviser John Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining classified information and faces up to five years in prison and a fine. CBS News' Jake Rosen has the latest.

item.title: Jalen Brunson's mom shares the story behind the nonprofit she cofounded with her son
item.description: Sandra Brunson, the mother of New York Knicks star Jalen Brunson, talks to CBS News chief medical correspondent Jon LaPook about the nonprofit she cofounded with her son, The Second Round Foundation, and the lessons she taught him about leadership.

item.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
item.description: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

item.title: How much interest can an $800 CD account earn this year?
item.description: Want to deposit a small amount into a CD while rates are high? Here's how much interest you can earn with $800 now.

item.title: Trump vows immediate 100% tariff if countries levy digital services tax
item.description: Countries that tax U.S. companies offering digital products and services would immediately face a 100% tariff on their exports to the U.S., President Trump said.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: U.S. men's soccer players remain confident heading into knockout round despite loss to Turkey
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team isn't letting Thursday night's loss to Turkey get to them. Several players said they're not worried about their first loss in the World Cup and are focusing on the knockout round. CBS News' Nicole Valdez has the latest.

item.title: Essential American songs of the last 250 years
item.description: For the United States' 250th birthday, Sunday Morning asked dozens of notable Americans, from Jason Alexander and Ken Burns to Misty Copeland, what they considered to be our country's essential songs. This is the Essential American Songbook: 90 contributors and 250 songs. Here's a sample.

item.title: OpenAI considers delaying its IPO until next year, report says
item.description: OpenAI is considering delaying its IPO until next year after tracking SpaceX's stock market debut earlier this month, according to the New York Times. Barron's reporter Megan Leonhardt joins to discuss.

item.title: Major bills in limbo after House leaders cancel votes amid SAVE America Act standoff
item.description: House Republican leaders canceled Friday's planned votes after several conservative allies of President Trump threatened to block legislation over the SAVE America Act. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining classified information
item.description: Bolton agreed to pay a $2.25 million fine, pleading guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information.

item.title: Jalen Brunson's mom on teaching him to be a leader: "It's how you respond"
item.description: The Second Round Foundation, founded by Sandra and Jalen Brunson, aims to help provide young people with what they need to thrive in the future.

item.title: Newsom proposes federal billionaire tax and AI "public equity" fund
item.description: As California Gov. Gavin Newsom eyes a 2028 presidential bid, he's calling for a national tax on billionaires and a public stake in AI, though he opposes a state ballot measure to tax billionaires.

item.title: Big Tech is spending trillions on AI. Investors want proof it'll pay off.
item.description: Technology companies are betting trillions of dollars that consumers will open their wallets for AI services. But what if Big Tech is wrong?

item.title: U.S. team suffers first 2026 World Cup loss, heads to knockout round
item.description: The U.S. men's team had already clinched its spot in the Round of 32, the knockout round, with its 2-0 win over Australia on Friday.

item.title: Paris bans alcohol in public amid record-breaking heat wave
item.description: Authorities are attempting to reduce the pressure on hospitals as the city swelters under extreme heat.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela, hundreds dead or injured
item.description: Venezuela's acting president said the death toll from powerful twin earthquakes was likely to rise, as USGS modeling suggested thousands may have been killed.

item.title: Venezuelan airport outside Caracas reopens after back-to-back earthquakes
item.description: People in northern Venezuela are racing against time to pull survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings after a pair of powerful earthquakes hit the country on Wednesday. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides has the latest from Bogotá, Colombia. Then, CBS News meteorologist Darren Peck joins to examine the risk of aftershocks.

item.title: 6-alarm fire destroys old industrial building in Allentown, Pennsylvania
item.description: A massive fire in Allentown, Pennsylvania, forced nearby residents to evacuate their homes Wednesday night.

item.title: See the aftermath of the Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela on Wednesday, the first measuring a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5. The country's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, declared a state of emergency. Videos show the heavy damage in the capital of Caracas as rescue crews look for survivors.

item.title: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes hit Venezuela
item.description: Two strong earthquakes hit west of Venezuela's capital on Wednesday. Scientists are estimating high casualties and extensive damage. BBC News' Vanessa Silva has the latest from Caracas.
